ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_assessment: High quality, clearly legible text-based PDF. All fields are fully readable.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_assessment: High quality, clearly legible text-based PDF. All fields are fully readable.

lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate number 'SEG-26D-465836' and remarks field '7200228151' do not contain any LC-related terminology (L/C, Letter of Credit, Documentary Credit, etc.). Standard verification applies.

locations_found: Invoice footer block 1: 'Ericsson AB' with VAT No: SE556056625801
Invoice footer block 2: 'Ericsson AB, Torshamnsgatan 23, S-164 80 STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN'
Invoice footer block 3: 'Ericsson AB S-164 80 Stockholm, Sweden Reg.Number:5560566258'
Invoice header: 'INVOICE' — issued by Ericsson AB as identifiable from footer
consignor_search_all_files.motivation: The consignor name 'ERICSSON AB' from the certificate is found explicitly multiple times in the invoice: in the footer address block ('Ericsson AB, Torshamnsgatan 23, S-164 80 STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN'), in the VAT block ('Ericsson AB, VAT No: SE556056625801'), and in the registration block ('Ericsson AB S-164 80 Stockholm, Sweden'). The trademark column also shows 'Ericsson'. Clear identification.

criteria_met: Criterion 2: Consignor 'ERICSSON AB' is identifiable in the invoice footer blocks.
Criterion 3: Consignee 'AZERCELL TELECOM LLC' is identifiable in the invoice 'Consignee/Ship to', 'Invoice to', and 'Customer/Sold to' fields.
pairing_validation.motivation: Pairing validation passed. Both consignor (ERICSSON AB) and consignee (AZERCELL TELECOM LLC) can be identified in the invoice. Additionally, the shipment number 7200228151 from certificate remarks matches the invoice shipment number.

certificate_fields.raw_text:
2 PACKAGES
TELECOM EQUIPMENT
PL - Router 6000 HW
certificate_fields.normalized_text: 2 packages telecom equipment pl - router 6000 hw

checkpoints.invoice_value: Ericsson AB, Torshamnsgatan 23, S-164 80 STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN (footer block); VAT No: SE556056625801

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name 'ERICSSON AB' anywhere in the invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: The name 'Ericsson AB' is found explicitly in the invoice footer blocks: (1) 'Ericsson AB VAT No: SE556056625801', (2) 'Ericsson AB Torshamnsgatan 23 S-164 80 STOCKHOLM SWEDEN', (3) 'Ericsson AB S-164 80 Stockholm, Sweden Reg.Number:5560566258'. The invoice is issued by Ericsson AB. Name match is exact after case normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that the country 'SWEDEN' from the certificate can be identified for the same party in the invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: The invoice footer block explicitly states 'SWEDEN' in the address 'Torshamnsgatan 23, S-164 80 STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN'. Additionally, VAT prefix 'SE' in 'SE556056625801' confirms Sweden per rule 4.1.2.1.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found explicitly in invoice footer/identification blocks.
Land verification via indirect identifiers – VAT prefix SE confirms Sweden.
Country check – SWEDEN in certificate matches SWEDEN in invoice.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'ERICSSON AB, TORSHAMNSGATAN 23, 164 80, STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN'. Invoice issuer (from footer): 'Ericsson AB, Torshamnsgatan 23, S-164 80 STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN'. Name match: 'ERICSSON AB' = 'Ericsson AB' after case normalization. Address match: 'TORSHAMNSGATAN 23, 164 80, STOCKHOLM' = 'Torshamnsgatan 23, S-164 80 STOCKHOLM' (S- prefix is a postal notation variant, not a substantive difference). Country: 'SWEDEN' = 'SWEDEN'. VAT prefix 'SE' additionally confirms Sweden. Full match on name, address, and country. Rule 4.1.0 gives MATCH as the consignor name is explicitly found in invoice identification blocks.

checkpoints.certificate_value: AZERCELL TELECOM LLC, TBILISI AVENUE 61A, 1122 BAKU, AZERBAIJAN
checkpoints.invoice_value: AZERCELL TELECOM LLC, TBILISI AVENUE 61A, 1122, BAKU, AZERBAIJAN (Invoice to field)

comparison_steps.step: 4.2.0.2 - Priority 1: Invoice to / Bill to field
comparison_steps.description: Identify the primary billing party in the invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: The 'Invoice to' field contains 'AZERCELL TELECOM LLC, TBILISI AVENUE 61A, 1122, BAKU, AZERBAIJAN'. This matches the certificate consignee exactly.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that the consignee company name from the certificate can be identified in the invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: 'AZERCELL TELECOM LLC' in certificate = 'AZERCELL TELECOM LLC' in invoice 'Invoice to' field. Exact match.
Certificate: 'AZERBAIJAN'. Invoice 'Invoice to' address: 'AZERBAIJAN'. Exact match.

rules_applied.description: Priority order for consignee identification – Invoice to field used as primary.
Name verification – exact match after normalization.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'AZERCELL TELECOM LLC, TBILISI AVENUE 61A, 1122 BAKU, AZERBAIJAN'. Invoice 'Invoice to': 'AZERCELL TELECOM LLC, TBILISI AVENUE 61A, 1122, BAKU, AZERBAIJAN'. The company name is identical. The address is identical (minor comma formatting difference only). Country is identical: AZERBAIJAN. Additionally confirmed by 'Consignee/Ship to' and 'Customer/Sold to' fields, all showing the same party. Clear MATCH.

checkpoints.certificate_value:
2 PACKAGES
TELECOM EQUIPMENT
PL - Router 6000 HW
checkpoints.invoice_value: Goods Marking: Telecommunication Equipment; Description: Router 6000 HW; Number of packages: 2 BOXES

comparison_steps.description: Check if article numbers are specified in the certificate.
comparison_steps.detail: The certificate does not specify a formal article/product code number. The text 'PL - Router 6000 HW' is a goods description, not a standalone article number. The invoice has product code 'FAP1304878' but this is not referenced in the certificate. Article number matching is not applicable.

comparison_steps.description: Identify the identity-bearing main designation in the certificate and search for it in the invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: The identity-bearing main designation in the certificate is 'Router 6000 HW'. The invoice product line 'Description' column contains 'Router 6000 HW'. This is an exact match. Additionally, the certificate states 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT' and the invoice 'Goods Marking' field contains 'Telecommunication Equipment'. 'TELECOM' vs 'Telecommunication' — 'TELECOM' is a widely recognized abbreviation of 'Telecommunication'. However, the primary verification is on 'Router 6000 HW' which matches exactly. The broader category 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT' vs 'Telecommunication Equipment' serves as corroborating context.

comparison_steps.description: Check for additions that do not affect product identity.
comparison_steps.detail: The 'PL -' prefix before 'Router 6000 HW' in the certificate is a country-of-origin code prefix (Poland), not an identity-bearing part of the product name. Per rule 4.3.4, geographic/country designations that correspond to the certificate's stated country of origin are non-identity-bearing additions. The '2 PACKAGES' in the certificate corresponds to '2 BOXES' in the invoice — this is a packaging descriptor, not an identity-bearing product designation.

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching – identity-bearing main designation 'Router 6000 HW' found exactly in invoice.
Non-identity-bearing additions – 'PL -' prefix and '2 PACKAGES' treated as non-identity-bearing.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate goods description: '2 PACKAGES / TELECOM EQUIPMENT / PL - Router 6000 HW'. The identity-bearing main designation is 'Router 6000 HW'. Invoice Description column: 'Router 6000 HW' — exact match. The broader category 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT' corresponds to invoice 'Goods Marking: Telecommunication Equipment' (TELECOM is a standard abbreviation of Telecommunication). The 'PL' prefix in the certificate corresponds to the stated Country of Origin (PL/Poland), treated as a non-identity-bearing geographic prefix per 4.3.4. Package count: certificate '2 PACKAGES', invoice '2 BOXES' — both indicate 2 packages, packaging type descriptor difference does not affect product identity. MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because 'TELECOM' in certificate differs from 'Telecommunication' in invoice.
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The primary identity-bearing designation 'Router 6000 HW' matches exactly. 'TELECOM' is a universally recognized abbreviation of 'Telecommunication' and appears in the broader goods category, not in the identity-bearing product name. Moreover, even if this difference existed, the exact match on 'Router 6000 HW' is sufficient for verification.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that the certificate provides both numeric value and unit.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate box 7 states 'G.W. 43,000 KG'. Numeric value: 43,000. Unit: KG. Weight category: G.W. (Gross Weight). All required elements are present.

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 contains 'G.W. 43,000 KG'. This is the verification-grounding quantity per rule 4.4.2.1.
comparison_steps.detail: Box 7 value 'G.W. 43,000 KG' is used as the verification basis.

comparison_steps.description: Normalize the numeric values for comparison. Certificate: '43,000' — the comma here functions as a thousands separator (43000). Invoice: '43.000' — the period functions as a thousands separator (43000).
comparison_steps.detail: Both values represent the integer 43000 after removing thousands separators. Certificate uses comma (43,000 = 43000 KG). Invoice uses period (43.000 = 43000 KG). Both are whole numbers with thousands separators. Normalization per 4.4.3.3 is valid: both values are integers, separators occur only as thousands separators.

comparison_steps.description: Verify that the numeric value 43000 KG GW can be identified in the invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice 'Total gross weight' field: '43.000 KG' = 43000 KG. Certificate: 'G.W. 43,000 KG' = 43000 KG GW. Numeric value matches exactly (43000). Unit matches (KG). Weight category: certificate specifies G.W. (Gross Weight), invoice field is labeled 'Total gross weight'. Category matches.

rules_applied.outcome: G.W. 43,000 KG identified as verification basis

rules_applied.description: Thousands separator normalization – comma in certificate and period in invoice both treated as thousands separators for the integer 43000.
Occurrence check – 43000 KG GW found in invoice Total gross weight field.
Total weight in invoice – 'Total gross weight: 43.000 KG' verified against certificate G.W.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: 'G.W. 43,000 KG'. This means Gross Weight 43,000 KG. The comma is a thousands separator (value = 43000 KG). Invoice 'Total gross weight: 43.000 KG'. The period is a thousands separator (value = 43000 KG). Per rule 4.4.3.3, thousands separator normalization is allowed when both values are integers and separators function only as thousands separators. Both conditions are met. After normalization: certificate = 43000 KG GW, invoice = 43000 KG Total gross weight. Values are identical. Weight category matches (GW = Total gross weight). MATCH. Note: Invoice also shows 'Total net weight: 27.209 KG' — this does not contradict the GW value; it is an additional weight category. Per rule 4.4.3.1, verifying at least one quantity (GW) is sufficient and the NW is not contradicted (certificate doesn't state NW).

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because '43,000' could mean 43.000 (forty-three) in some locales.
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: In the context of this certificate, '43,000 KG' clearly uses comma as a thousands separator. A single package with gross weight of merely 43 grams (0.043 KG) would be inconsistent with '2 PACKAGES' of 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT / Router 6000 HW'. The invoice confirms the value as 43.000 KG (43000 grams = 43 KG) which is consistent. Both conventions represent the same numeric value: 43 kilograms (43.000 KG). Actually, reviewing more carefully: 43.000 KG in the invoice with period as thousands separator would mean 43000 KG, not 43 KG. But the invoice also says 'Total net weight: 27.209 KG'. If period were thousands separator, NW would be 27209 KG which is unreasonable for 2 boxes of telecom equipment. Therefore: in the invoice, the period is a DECIMAL separator: 43.000 KG = exactly 43 KG, and 27.209 KG = 27.209 KG. Re-evaluating: In the certificate, '43,000 KG' — comma as thousands separator would mean 43000 KG. But if it's a decimal, it means 43.000 KG = 43 KG exactly. Given the invoice net weight is 27.209 KG (clearly using period as decimal with 3 decimal places), the invoice gross weight 43.000 KG = 43 KG exactly (3 decimal places, all zeros). The certificate '43,000 KG' = 43 KG exactly (using comma as decimal separator with 3 decimal places, all zeros). Both = 43.000 KG. MATCH regardless of separator interpretation. The key point: both represent exactly 43 kilograms.

comparison_steps.description: Search for an explicit Country of Origin field in the invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: The invoice product line contains a dedicated 'CoO' column (Country of Origin). For the single product line (PO Item 00150), the CoO value is 'PL'.

comparison_steps.description: Compare certificate origin 'PL' with invoice origin 'PL'.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate box 3: 'PL'. Invoice CoO column: 'PL'. Exact match. PL = Poland (ISO alpha-2 code). No normalization needed.

comparison_steps.description: Check if EU normalization is relevant.
comparison_steps.detail: The certificate header indicates 'EUROPEAN UNION' and the issuing stamp is from Sweden. The certificate title is an EU Certificate of Origin. However, box 3 (Country of origin) specifically states 'PL' (Poland). Poland is an EU member state. The invoice CoO column also states 'PL'. Direct match on 'PL' = 'PL'. EU normalization is not needed but would also be consistent: PL is an EU member state. The manufacturer listed in the invoice is 'FLEXTRONICS INTERNATIONAL POLAND SP ZOO' which corroborates Polish origin.

rules_applied.description: Identification of origin in invoice – CoO column found with value 'PL'.
Normalization – exact ISO code match, no normalization needed.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 (Country of origin): 'PL'. Invoice CoO column for the product line: 'PL'. These are identical ISO alpha-2 country codes for Poland. Direct exact match. The invoice manufacturer 'FLEXTRONICS INTERNATIONAL POLAND SP ZOO' further corroborates Polish origin, though this corroboration is not required for the verification. MATCH.

evidence_map.description: Country of origin in certificate box 3
Country of origin in invoice CoO column

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: No LC reference found in certificate. Standard verification applies.
Document A identified as certificate (EU Certificate of Origin, No. SEG-26D-465836). Document B identified as invoice (Invoice No. 9209876396). Single certificate, single invoice. Clear pairing.

PASSED. Consignor ERICSSON AB found in invoice footer. Consignee AZERCELL TELECOM LLC found in invoice Invoice-to field. Shipment number 7200228151 matches between certificate remarks and invoice shipment number.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name search in all invoice pages
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MATCH. 'ERICSSON AB' found explicitly in invoice footer blocks.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Land verification via indirect identifiers (VAT prefix)
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: VAT prefix SE in SE556056625801 confirms Sweden.
Invoice-to field identified as primary. AZERCELL TELECOM LLC found.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Controlled text matching for goods description
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MATCH. Identity-bearing designation 'Router 6000 HW' found exactly in invoice.
'PL -' prefix treated as geographic/origin code, not identity-bearing.
G.W. 43,000 KG used as verification basis.

Certificate 43,000 and invoice 43.000 both normalized to 43000. However, on further analysis both represent 43.000 KG (43 kilograms exactly with 3 decimal places). Either interpretation yields the same numeric value.
MATCH. 43.000 KG GW found in invoice Total gross weight.
MATCH. Total gross weight field used.
CoO column found with value PL.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter stämmer överens mellan certifikatet och fakturan. Avsändaren Ericsson AB med adress i Stockholm, Sverige, mottagaren Azercell Telecom LLC i Baku, Azerbajdzjan, varubeskrivningen Router 6000 HW, bruttovikten 43,000 kg och ursprungslandet Polen (PL) kan alla verifieras mot fakturan. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: All five checkpoints (consignor, consignee, goods description, quantity, country of origin) verified as MATCH. Certificate No. SEG-26D-465836 issued 2026-03-02 for Ericsson AB. Invoice No. 9209876396 dated 08-01-2026. Consignor Ericsson AB (Sweden) verified via invoice footer. Consignee Azercell Telecom LLC (Azerbaijan) verified via Invoice-to field. Goods 'Router 6000 HW' exact match. Gross weight 43.000 KG exact match. Origin PL exact match.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.invoice_value: Ericsson AB, Torshamnsgatan 23, S-164 80 STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN (footer); VAT No: SE556056625801
sections.rule_applied: Koncernstruktur (4.1.0) – consignor-namn identifierat i fakturans identifieringsblock. Landverifiering via explicit adress och VAT-prefix SE.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'ERICSSON AB' med adress 'TORSHAMNSGATAN 23, 164 80, STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN' återfinns uttryckligen i fakturans footer-block som 'Ericsson AB, Torshamnsgatan 23, S-164 80 STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN'. Namnmatchning är exakt efter normalisering av versaler. Adress överensstämmer (S-prefix i postnummer är en postnotationsvariant). Land SWEDEN bekräftas direkt i adressen samt indirekt via VAT-prefix SE.

sections.certificate_value: AZERCELL TELECOM LLC, TBILISI AVENUE 61A, 1122 BAKU, AZERBAIJAN
sections.invoice_value: AZERCELL TELECOM LLC, TBILISI AVENUE 61A, 1122, BAKU, AZERBAIJAN (Invoice to)
sections.rule_applied: Primärt faktureringsfält (4.2.0.2) – Invoice-to-fält identifierat och använt. Namnverifiering (4.2.2) – exakt matchning.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'AZERCELL TELECOM LLC' med adress 'TBILISI AVENUE 61A, 1122 BAKU, AZERBAIJAN' matchas exakt mot fakturans 'Invoice to'-fält som anger 'AZERCELL TELECOM LLC, TBILISI AVENUE 61A, 1122, BAKU, AZERBAIJAN'. Företagsnamn, adress och land överensstämmer helt.

sections.certificate_value:
2 PACKAGES
TELECOM EQUIPMENT
PL - Router 6000 HW
sections.invoice_value: Description: Router 6000 HW; Goods Marking: Telecommunication Equipment; 2 BOXES
sections.rule_applied: Kontrollerad textmatchning (4.3.3) – identitetsbärande huvudbeteckning 'Router 6000 HW' identifierad exakt i fakturan. Icke-identitetsbärande tillägg (4.3.4) – 'PL -' prefix behandlat som geografisk kod.
sections.motivation: Den identitetsbärande huvudbeteckningen i certifikatet är 'Router 6000 HW'. Denna återfinns exakt i fakturans beskrivningskolumn som 'Router 6000 HW'. Certifikatets bredare kategori 'TELECOM EQUIPMENT' stöds av fakturans 'Goods Marking: Telecommunication Equipment'. 'PL -' prefixet i certifikatet avser ursprungskoden och är inte identitetsbärande.

sections.rule_applied: Förekomstkontroll (4.4.1) med tusentalsavskiljarnormalisering (4.4.3.3). Box 7 som verifieringsgrund (4.4.2.1). Totalvikt i faktura (4.4.4).
sections.motivation: Certifikatets box 7 anger 'G.W. 43,000 KG' (bruttovikt 43,000 kg). Fakturan anger 'Total gross weight: 43.000 KG'. Båda representerar exakt 43 kilogram (43,000 med komma som decimalavskiljare = 43.000 med punkt som decimalavskiljare). Numeriskt värde, enhet (KG) och viktkategori (Gross Weight) överensstämmer exakt.

sections.rule_applied: Ursprungsidentifiering (4.5.1) – dedikerad CoO-kolumn i fakturan. Normalisering (4.5.4) – exakt ISO-kodmatchning.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets box 3 anger ursprungsland 'PL' (Polen). Fakturans CoO-kolumn (Country of Origin) i produktraden anger 'PL'. Exakt matchning av ISO alfa-2-landskod. Tillverkaren 'FLEXTRONICS INTERNATIONAL POLAND SP ZOO' stödjer ytterligare polskt ursprung.

human_readable_report.conclusion: Samtliga fem kontrollpunkter (avsändare, mottagare, varubeskrivning, kvantitet, ursprungsland) har verifierats som MATCH. Certifikatet kan verifieras mot fakturan. Rekommendation: automatiskt godkännande.

output_quality_checks.notes: All applicable checkpoints have confidence >= 0.85 and status MATCH. Comparison result IDENTICAL is consistent. Workflow recommendation AUTO_APPROVAL_ELIGIBLE is consistent with IDENTICAL result.
